questions.question: Your MailReach inbox score drops from 88 to 64 overnight. What's your first action?

options.text: Immediately pause all sends from that inbox
Increase warmup volume to rebuild reputation

questions.explanation: Always pause first when placement drops >20 points. Prevents further damage while you diagnose the root cause.

questions.question: GlockApps shows 'DKIM: FAIL' and inbox placement dropped to 15%. What's the issue?

options.text: Your email content has spam triggers
Your DNS DKIM record is broken or missing

questions.explanation: DKIM failure is always a DNS configuration issue. Check your DKIM record in MXToolbox and regenerate if needed.

questions.question: Google Postmaster shows your spam complaint rate is 0.22%. What should you do?

options.text: Reduce volume by 50% immediately and investigate list quality
Increase volume to dilute the complaint rate

questions.explanation: 0.22% is in the danger zone (Google blocks at 0.3%). Reduce volume immediately and clean your list to prevent domain damage.

questions.question: You increased from 30/day to 75/day per inbox and placement dropped from 85% to 58%. What happened?
questions.explanation: Sudden volume increases (>20% in one day) trigger ISP suspicion. Roll back to previous volume and ramp slowly (2-3 emails/day increase).

questions.question: Your bounce rate jumped from 2% to 14% overnight. What's the most likely cause?

options.text: Bad list data (old emails or spam traps)

questions.explanation: Sudden bounce rate spikes indicate list quality issues — old data, purchased lists, or spam traps. Clean your list immediately.

questions.question: How often should you run GlockApps tests during the warmup phase?
questions.explanation: During warmup, test daily to catch issues early. At cruise speed, 2-3x per week is sufficient.

questions.question: Gmail inbox placement is 88% but Outlook is 22%. What's the issue?

options.text: Content spam triggers (Outlook filters are stricter)

questions.explanation: Provider-specific placement differences indicate content issues. Outlook penalizes links, HTML, and spam words more heavily than Gmail.

questions.question: What's the safe maximum increase in daily sending volume per inbox?
questions.explanation: Safe ramp: 2-3 emails/day increase every 2-3 days. Faster increases trigger ISP filters and damage reputation.

questions.question: Google Postmaster domain reputation dropped from 'High' to 'Medium'. How long does recovery typically take?
questions.explanation: Reputation recovery takes 7-14 days of reduced volume, improved engagement, and clean list hygiene. Patience is critical.

questions.question: What's the minimum daily volume needed for Google Postmaster to show domain reputation data?
questions.explanation: Google Postmaster requires 100+ daily emails to Gmail addresses to display domain reputation. Below that, you won't see data.
